description: PromQL feature showcase: one panel target per promql-feature-inventory.json row, enforced by test/inventory. Panels carry cerberus.expect contracts where the defined outcome is empty or an error.

panels.description: Bare instant selector plus positive and negative offsets. The rolling re-seeder keeps a ±5m window around now, so both offset directions resolve against real samples.
panels.title: Instant selector + offset / negative offset

panels.description: The @ timestamp modifier with the start()/end() functions resolved against the query_range window.
panels.title: @ start() / @ end() modifiers

panels.description: The canonical Grafana subquery idiom: an outer range reducer over an inner rate() matrix.
panels.title: Subquery: max_over_time over rate

panels.description: Counter-rate family over the seeded monotone request counter.
panels.title: rate / increase / irate

panels.description: Gauge delta family over the seeded flapping gauge plus a least-squares derivative over the monotone counter.
panels.title: delta / idelta / deriv

panels.description: showcase_restarting_total is seeded as a sawtooth (resets every 150s), so every 5m window contains at least one reset.
panels.title: resets on a sawtooth counter

panels.description: showcase_flapping alternates 0/1 every 15s sample, so changes() is always non-zero.
panels.title: changes on a flapping gauge

panels.description: Linear extrapolation over the monotone counter.
panels.title: predict_linear

panels.description: The basic *_over_time reducers over the seeded up gauge.
panels.title: avg / min / max / sum / count over_time

panels.description: The statistical *_over_time reducers. mad_over_time now renders live in the 'Statistical & timestamp over-time' row (lowering + chsql median-of-abs-deviation reducer landed).
panels.title: stddev / stdvar / quantile over_time

panels.description: The window-edge selector. first_over_time / present_over_time live in the parity-rejection row until their emitter support lands.
panels.title: last_over_time

panels.description: Unary math over the up gauge.
panels.title: abs / ceil / floor / round / sqrt

panels.description: Exponential/log family. The log arguments select the always-1 api series (shifted where needed) so every sample stays in-domain.
panels.title: exp / ln / log2 / log10 / sgn

panels.description: The clamp family with scalar-literal bounds.
panels.title: clamp / clamp_min / clamp_max

panels.description: Label rewriting over the seeded multi-label series (color / shape / env).
panels.title: label_replace / label_join

panels.description: Synthetic value functions: wall-clock seconds, per-sample timestamps, and a constant vector.
panels.title: time / timestamp / vector

panels.description: Calendar decomposition of each sample's timestamp.
panels.title: calendar functions

panels.description: Cerberus folds step() to the query_range step in seconds (0 for an instant query) — a query-context constant rendered per step.
panels.title: step()

panels.description: Cerberus folds range() to (end - start) in seconds — a query-context constant rendered per step across the query_range window.
panels.title: range()

panels.description: The basic aggregators with a by(job) clause over the two seeded up series.
panels.title: sum / avg / min / max by (job)

panels.description: Counting/grouping aggregators with without(), statistical aggregators with by().
panels.title: count / group without, stddev / stdvar by

panels.description: Ranking aggregators selecting the highest/lowest of the two up series.
panels.title: topk / bottomk

panels.description: Quantile across series and the value-histogram aggregator over the multi-label gauge.
panels.title: quantile / count_values

panels.description: Cerberus lowers PromQL's experimental `limitk(K, v)` aggregator to ClickHouse's `LIMIT K BY <group>` (no ORDER BY): up to K arbitrary series per group, samples unchanged.
panels.title: limitk

cerberus.why: Cerberus implements this construct; the panel asserts a live nonempty result. Previously a parity-rejection pin — the experimental-aggregator burndown landed the lowering (limitk → LIMIT K BY), so the contract is upgraded to nonempty per the bidirectional ratchet.

panels.description: All six arithmetic operators, vector-vector and vector-scalar.
panels.title: arithmetic operators

panels.description: Comparison operators in filter form. Each matcher keeps at least one of the seeded up series (api=1, db=0).
panels.title: comparison operators (filter form)

panels.description: The bool modifier turns the comparison into a 0/1 valued vector — both seeded series survive.
panels.title: comparison with bool modifier

panels.description: Set operators. The unless arm subtracts the db series, keeping api — a nonempty defined result.
panels.title: set operators: and / or / unless

panels.description: Explicit matching-label control: on(job) for the identical-label case, ignoring(http_status) to bridge the counter's extra label.
panels.title: vector matching: on / ignoring

panels.description: Many-to-one and one-to-many matching across the counter and the up gauge.
panels.title: vector matching: group_left / group_right

panels.description: The classic-histogram quantile idiom over the seeded request-duration histogram's bucket companions.
panels.title: classic histogram_quantile over _bucket rates

panels.description: The _exp_hist suffix routes the selector onto cerberus's native (exponential) histogram quantile lowering against otel_metrics_exp_histogram.
panels.title: native histogram_quantile over an exponential histogram

panels.description: The _count/_sum companion routing onto the histogram table's Count/Sum columns.
panels.title: histogram companions: _count / _sum rates

panels.description: absent() over a metric that IS seeded is defined to return no samples. Declared empty so the sweep pins the defined-empty direction; the sibling panel pins the inverse.
panels.title: absent(up) — defined empty

cerberus.why: absent() over the seeded `up` metric is DEFINED to return no samples while the metric keeps reporting; the sibling panel (absent of a never-seeded name) pins the inverse direction, so both sides of the absence contract are enforced.

panels.description: The metric name is never seeded anywhere, so both absence functions return the constant 1 series — the nonempty direction of the absence contract.
panels.title: absent / absent_over_time of a never-seeded name

panels.description: Cerberus implements `acos(up)` (surface burndown #65).
panels.title: acos

cerberus.why: Cerberus implements this construct; the panel asserts a live nonempty result. Previously a parity-rejection pin — the surface burndown (#65) landed the lowering, so the contract is upgraded to nonempty per the bidirectional ratchet.

panels.description: Parity pin for the upstream Prometheus 11,000-points-per-series query_range cap (#744): the sweep honours this target's 1ms min interval, producing a ~300k-point grid that cerberus MUST reject with the upstream error message.
panels.title: query_range resolution cap (>11k points) — parity rejection

cerberus.why: Pins the #744 parity contract: cerberus mirrors upstream Prometheus's 11,000-points-per-timeseries query_range cap. The probe honours the target's 1ms min interval, producing a grid far over the cap; success here would mean the cap regressed.

panels.description: double_exponential_smoothing (a.k.a. holt_winters) is an experimental PromQL function reference Prometheus rejects without the experimental-functions feature flag; the PromQL head gates it for strict reference parity.
panels.title: double_exponential_smoothing — parity rejection

cerberus.why: Cerberus rejects this construct today; the declaration is a bidirectional parity pin — the moment lowering support lands, this panel fails the sweep and the contract must be upgraded to nonempty.

panels.description: Median absolute deviation over a 5m window — median(|x - median(x)|).
panels.title: mad_over_time

cerberus.why: Cerberus implements this construct (lowering + chsql reducer landed); the panel asserts a live nonempty result. Previously a parity-rejection pin in the collapsed rejection row — the moment lowering/emitter support landed, the contract was upgraded to nonempty and the panel moved into the over-time showcase row.
